name: Orange bell pepper (food medium)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 170108 (Peppers, sweet, red, raw) via nutritionvalue.org
components.evidence: Glucose 1.94 g/100g; orange bell pepper is a fully-ripe Capsicum annuum compositionally equivalent to red sweet pepper. Glucose+fructose are the dominant carbon/energy source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Fructose 2.26 g/100g; fructose > glucose in ripe sweet pepper. Sugars increase and become the major soluble solids as the fruit ripens to orange/red.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Sucrose ~0 g/100g in ripe sweet pepper; hexoses dominate. Trace sucrose (<0.1 g) reported in some cultivars.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2023 green pepper (Capsicum annuum), PMC9871545
components.evidence: Citric acid is the dominant organic acid, 1.82-5.60 mg/g FW (183-560 mg/100g); midpoint ~370 mg/100g used. Citric increases with ripening, so ripe orange fruit is at the higher end. Primary organic-acid carbon source.

components.name: L-malic acid
components.evidence: Malic 0.99-1.88 mg/g FW (99-188 mg/100g); midpoint ~140 mg/100g. Malic declines with ripening (upper range is unripe green).

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Reported as second-most-abundant acid, 1.48-3.71 mg/g FW. Conservative lower-range value used (medium confidence; fumaric is unusually high in this dataset relative to other pepper studies).

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Minor acid, <0.7 mg/g FW (<70 mg/100g), <8% of total organic acids; ~40 mg/100g estimate.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Ascorbic acid 128 mg/100g (red/orange sweet pepper is among the most vitamin-C-rich vegetables; ripe fruit 120-180 mg/100g). Acts as both vitamin and organic acid.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Total protein 0.99 g/100g; amino-acid profile below is the protein-derived composition.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (incl. free asparagine pool); a preferred N/C source.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Second most abundant amino acid.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral.

components.name: phosphate (P)
components.evidence: Phosphorus 26 mg/100g.

components.name: molybdate (Mo)
components.evidence: Genuine plant micronutrient (cofactor for nitrate reductase); present at trace ug levels, unquantified in USDA. Kept, not noise.

components.name: boron (B)
components.evidence: Essential plant micronutrient (~0.1-0.3 mg/100g typical in vegetables); kept as real, not contaminant.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Genuine counter-ion; unquantified.

components.name: sulfate (S)
components.evidence: Sulfur present via cysteine/methionine and inorganic sulfate; kept.

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: Listed also as ascorbate organic acid above; retained here as the defining vitamin.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: DFE.

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Absent (plant food).

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: From provitamin-A carotenoids; orange fruit rich in beta-carotene/beta-cryptoxanthin.

components.name: vitamin D
components.evidence: Absent.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: ~5.5 mg/100g in sweet pepper.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Low-fat matrix.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant PUFA.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1)
components.evidence: Total MUFA 0.0067 g/100g.

components.name: fiber (total)
components.evidence: Largely non-fermentable structural polysaccharide.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin-A carotenoid; contributes orange color. Real, in BiGG. Quantitatively cultivar-dependent (orange fruit hundreds of ug/100g).

components.name: zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Zeaxanthin (and its esters) is the signature pigment giving orange bell peppers their color; the dominant carotenoid in orange cultivars. Real.

components.name: lutein
components.evidence: Real xanthophyll carotenoid.

components.name: beta-cryptoxanthin
components.evidence: Provitamin-A xanthophyll, genuine.

components.name: alpha-carotene
components.evidence: Genuine carotenoid, minor.

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: Synthetic pharmaceutical (contraceptive estrogen); biologically impossible in a plant food. FooDB predicted/contaminant artifact.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant metal, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: Toxic trace contaminant, not a nutrient.

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: Toxic heavy-metal contaminant.

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: Toxic heavy-metal contaminant.

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: Toxic heavy-metal contaminant.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant (not required by the model matrix).

remove.name: Co2+ / cobalt
remove.reason: Trace contaminant; only relevant as B12 cofactor which plants lack. Drop.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element, FooDB noise for a plant matrix.

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant metal.

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant metal.

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant metal.

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element, passenger of K uptake; not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Bromine
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace halogen contaminant.

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: Non-essential trace element for this matrix; FooDB noise.

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: Animal muscle metabolite; implausible in a plant food. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Taurine
remove.reason: Animal-derived amino sulfonic acid; implausible in a plant food. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Dopamine
remove.reason: Catecholamine present only at trace/unmeasured levels in pepper; FooDB predicted, not a growth nutrient. Removed.

remove.name: Putrescine
remove.reason: Polyamine at trace levels; FooDB predicted, negligible as a growth substrate.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer implausible as a bulk plant metabolite; FooDB stereochemistry artifact (real form is L-serine).

remove.name: D-Ornithine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer artifact; the biological form is L-ornithine and it is a trace intermediate.

remove.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
remove.reason: Oxidation artifact of methionine, not a distinct nutrient pool.

remove.name: L-Citrulline
remove.reason: Trace non-protein amino acid, FooDB predicted; negligible pool in pepper (unlike watermelon).

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: Non-nutrient/contaminant aromatic amine; FooDB predicted.

remove.name: p-Aminobenzaldehyde
remove.reason: Non-nutrient aromatic aldehyde; FooDB predicted artifact.

remove.name: Piperidine
remove.reason: Trace volatile alkaloid, not a growth nutrient; FooDB predicted.

remove.name: NG,NG-Dimethyl-L-arginine (ADMA)
remove.reason: Animal-associated methylarginine metabolite; implausible/trace in plant. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Racemethionine
remove.reason: Racemic (DL) methionine is a synthetic-feed descriptor, not a native metabolite; the native form (L-methionine) is retained.

missing_added: D-glucose and D-fructose with individual amounts (were absent; hexoses are the dominant carbon source)
citric acid (dominant organic acid, ~370 mg/100g) — was entirely missing
L-malic acid, fumaric acid, alpha-ketoglutaric acid, oxalic acid — organic acids missing
full mineral panel with amounts (K, P, Mg, Ca, Na, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se)
full vitamin panel with amounts (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9, C, A, E, K)
amino-acid amounts (were name-only) and total protein
fatty-acid amounts (linoleic, alpha-linolenic, oleic, SFA) and total fat, fiber, water, choline
keep_uncovered: Capsaicin, Dihydrocapsaicin, Nordihydrocapsaicin, Homocapsaicin, Homodihydrocapsaicin, Nonivamide, Capsiamide (capsaicinoids/amides — genuine but very low in sweet non-pungent bell pepper; no BiGG met)
Capsianosides I-IV / A-F, Capsicoside A1/B1/C1 (genuine Capsicum glycosides; no BiGG met)
Luteolin, Caffeic acid, p-Coumaric acid, Moupinamide (real phenolics/hydroxycinnamates; no BiGG met)
Solanidine, Capsidiol (genuine Capsicum steroidal alkaloid / phytoalexin; no BiGG met)
zeaxanthin, lutein, beta-carotene, beta-cryptoxanthin, alpha-carotene (genuine carotenoids; kept in components as phytochemicals)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170108 (Peppers, sweet, red, raw) — full nutrient/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel; used as proxy for fully-ripe orange bell pepper
USDA FoodData Central FDC 170432 (Peppers, sweet, yellow, raw) — cross-check for ripe non-red cultivar
Zhang et al. 2023, 'Comprehensive fruit quality assessment ... green pepper (Capsicum annuum L.)', PMC9871545 — organic acid HPLC quantification
Nature Sci Rep 2026 s41598-026-37078-w — comparative nutrient & organic-acid profiles of pepper genotypes (corroborating organic-acid ranges)
Matsufuji et al. 2007, Int J Food Sci Technol, doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.2006.01368.x — carotenoid/antioxidant content of orange vs other coloured sweet peppers
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are the free hexoses D-fructose (~2.3 g) and D-glucose (~1.9 g) plus citric acid (~0.37 g), with L-malic and ascorbate secondary; sucrose is negligible. Orange bell pepper is a fully-ripe Capsicum annuum, compositionally near red sweet pepper (used as the quantitative proxy), distinguished mainly by a zeaxanthin/beta-carotene-dominated carotenoid profile. Fumaric-acid value flagged medium-confidence (source reports it unusually high). Removed 28 items: heavy-metal/trace contaminants, an implausible pharmaceutical (ethynylestradiol), animal metabolites (creatine, taurine), D-enantiomer/oxidation artifacts, and FooDB-predicted non-nutrients.
